name: Combined: Slowloris Analysis (Testing + Iran 2009 + Archive 2024)
description: Combined view of Slowloris attacks across three scenarios: Laboratory testing, Iran 2009 protests, and Internet Archive 2024

techniques.comment:
[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Web Protocols: Attack uses legitimate HTTP protocol on port 80, sending partial headers to maintain connections

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Web Protocols: Strategic use of HTTP to maintain stealth and preserve bandwidth for protester communications during civil unrest

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Web Protocols: Sophisticated use of HTTP/HTTPS with modern evasion techniques to mimic legitimate traffic patterns

[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Service Exhaustion Flood: Exhausted Apache MaxRequestWorkers (all 150 slots filled) with 500 slow connections. AH00161 error logged.

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Service Exhaustion Flood: Multiple Iranian government websites rendered unavailable for hours/days during critical political period

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Service Exhaustion Flood: Massive-scale exhaustion of Internet Archive's web services - Extended offline period affecting global access

[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Application Exhaustion Flood: Targeted HTTP application layer by sending X-a: b headers every 10-20 seconds, keeping threads occupied indefinitely

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Application Exhaustion Flood: Targeted government web servers' HTTP connection pools to disrupt state propaganda dissemination

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Application Exhaustion Flood: Advanced application-layer attack targeting modern web architectures, load balancers, and CDN infrastructure

[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Vulnerabilities: Acquired Slowloris Python script from public repositories to exploit HTTP connection handling

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Vulnerabilities: Protesters distributed Slowloris tool and knowledge for coordinated campaign

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Vulnerabilities: Research into modern variations of Slowloris and other slow HTTP attacks to bypass contemporary defenses

[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Vulnerability Scanning: Identified Apache/2.4 web server running mpm_prefork (thread-based) without mod_reqtimeout protection

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Vulnerability Scanning: Activists identified Iranian government websites (gerdab.ir, leader.ir) as vulnerable targets

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Vulnerability Scanning: Advanced reconnaissance of Internet Archive's large-scale infrastructure, CDN, and load balancing systems

[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Web Services: Used distributed infrastructure across multiple protester machines to avoid self-inflicted network saturation

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Web Services: Established sophisticated botnet infrastructure with thousands of compromised systems for massive distributed assault

[Testing Scenario - Slowloris Attack] Multi-hop Proxy: Command used --proxy-host 127.0.0.1 --proxy-port 1080 to route attack through SOCKS proxy

[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Multi-hop Proxy: Used proxy networks and compromised systems to obscure attack sources and evade IP-based blocking
[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] External Defacement: Made world's largest digital library unavailable to public, affecting researchers, historians, and general users globally
[Internet Archive 2024 - Modern Slow DoS] Malware: Likely deployed custom/modified slow DoS tools and botnet malware for large-scale distributed attack
[Iran 2009 - Political Hacktivism] Internal Defacement: Silenced government communications by making internal propaganda websites inaccessible to Iranian citizens
